title: 國泰出聲恐嚇員工唔可以去今日機場集會!!!!!

replies.msg:
Dear colleagues,

First of all, let me say that I have been immensely impressed by the dedication and professionalism shown by the majority of our employees over the last two months in order to keep our operations going in such difficult circumstances.

You would have seen my message to you about the CAAC notice sent over the weekend. As I said in my message, we will comply with the CAAC notice in full.

I thought it important for me now to make a few more points.

We are all obliged to abide by law at all times. Cathay Pacific Group has a zero tolerance approach to illegal activities. Specifically, in the current context, there will be disciplinary consequences for employees who support or participate in illegal protests. These consequences could be serious and may include termination of employment.

I also want to touch on our conduct. We have a Code of Conduct as you know - it requires all of us as professionals to maintain our high standards. It also imposes policies, for example confidentiality and social media. We recently had employees disclosing confidential business information and that is a violation of the Code. That is just not acceptable.

As an additional point, it is important to remember that actions and words of our employees made outside of working hours can have a significant effect on the company - we have a high profile in the community given the nature of our business and inevitably actions of our employees frequently attract attention and would be treated as that representing the company’s position. Our Social Media Policy also makes it clear that you should not post anything that breaches privacy of colleagues, constitutes bullying or harassment or could bring Cathay Pacific Group into disrepute.

Possible protest at HKIA today

Lastly, I want to bring to your attention that media reports are suggesting that there may be a protest at the Hong Kong International Airport today. To the extent it has not been authorised by the authorities, the protest will be treated as illegal. It is therefore important that you do not support or participate in this protest. We would also be concerned about your safety if the protest becomes disorderly or violent.

We trust all of our employees to conduct themselves with the same levels of respect, care and professionalism for which they are known. The majority of you have been – let’s keep this up. Your continued hard work, can-do attitude and ongoing support for our customers and fellow colleagues are at the heart of what we all stand for at Cathay Pacific Group.

Thank you,

Rupert
